title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: Sunday, March 23, 2026: Markets Closed, Reviewing Friday's Performance and Weekend News

items.headline: Weekend Review: Geopolitical Tensions and Inflation Fears Dominate Market Outlook
items.summary: U.S. equity markets closed significantly lower on Friday, marking a fourth consecutive week of declines for major indices. Escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, particularly the ongoing conflict in Iran, continue to drive oil prices higher, fueling inflation concerns and prompting speculation about the Federal Reserve's monetary policy path. Investors are bracing for a volatile week ahead as markets grapple with the implications of persistent inflation and a potentially hawkish Fed.

items.statContext: Down 1.5% (100.01 points) on Friday, marking its fourth straight weekly decline.

items.headline: Tilly's surges over 167% in March
items.summary: Specialty retailer Tilly's, Inc. has been the top performer month-to-date.

items.headline: Sable Offshore continues strong performance
items.summary: Sable Offshore Corp. shows significant gains in March, driven by sector trends.

items.headline: VivoPower PLC plunges on corporate announcement
items.summary: VivoPower PLC experienced a sharp decline following its announcement regarding a new stock ticker and corporate name change.

items.headline: MI NFT Limited drops significantly
items.summary: NFT Ltd. saw a substantial loss after pricing a registered direct offering.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady Amid Inflation Concerns
items.summary: The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) decided to keep the federal funds rate unchanged at 3.50% to 3.75% during its March 18 meeting. The decision, largely anticipated by markets, comes as rising gas prices and a weakening labor market complicate the economic outlook. The Fed's latest projections still hint at one rate cut this year, despite an upward revision to inflation forecasts, reflecting ongoing concerns about price stability.

items.headline: Iran Conflict Escalates, Driving Oil Prices Higher and Fueling Stagflation Fears
items.summary: The ongoing conflict in Iran continues to be a major headwind for global markets, pushing Brent crude prices above $112 per barrel by Friday's close. The sustained surge in energy costs is intensifying inflation worries and contributing to a 'stagflation geometry' in the Treasury market, where growth expectations are rolling over while inflation remains hot. This geopolitical uncertainty is a key factor impacting market sentiment and future monetary policy.

items.headline: Energy and Financials Lead as Utilities and Basic Materials Lag Last Week
items.summary: For the week ending March 20, the energy sector emerged as a top performer, gaining 2.99%, followed by financial services, up 0.35%. In contrast, the utilities sector saw a significant decline of 5.0%, and basic materials fell 4.82%, indicating a flight to defensive sectors amid market volatility.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports Expected This Week
items.summary: While today, March 23, sees limited corporate earnings (Bioline RX Ltd, Lithium Argentina, MBX Biosciences), the upcoming week features highly anticipated reports. GameStop Corp. ($GME) is set to release its quarterly earnings on Tuesday, followed by pet retailer Chewy, Inc. ($CHWY) on Wednesday. Thursday will bring results from Commercial Metals Co., Shoe Carnival, Lovesac, and Lantern Pharma.
